content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA

FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
926 J Street, Suite 522
Sacramento 95814
(916) 445-5660

July 29, 1975

Ms. Margaret Bromley
County Clerk
County of Inyo
Drawer F
Independence, California 93526

Dear Ms. Bromley:

Re: Request for Opinion
No. 75-152

Thank you for your letter of July 9, 1975, requesting that the Fair Political Practices Commission issue an opinion on the question of whether a person who does not appear on the ballot is considered a candidate and must file campaign statements. Your request for a formal opinion is denied because your question does not raise a substantial question of interpretation of the Political Reform Act.

Section 82007 of the Government Code defines the term candidate as "an individual who is listed on the ballot; or who receives a contribution or makes an expenditure or gives his consent for any other person to receive a contribution or make an expenditure with a view to bringing about his nomination or election to any elective office..." Thus, the candidates described in your letter will not have to file campaign statements if they do not appear on the ballot and do not receive contributions or make expenditures.

Although for the reasons stated no formal opinion will be issued, I hope the foregoing is sufficiently helpful. If you have any questions, I urge you to contact a member of the Commission staff.

Sincerely,

Daniel H. Lowenstein
Chairman, for the
Commission

DHL:nc

EDMUND G. BROWN JR. Governor

Request No. 7-1352
Data Received 11/4/75
EUOFIndia Clark Board of Supervisors
Response Due 7/28/75 Ex Officio Clerk Superior Court
Registrar of Voters

Telephone
(714) 878-2411

COUNTY OF INYO
MARGARET (RITA) BROMLEY
CLERK—RECORDER
COURTHOUSE
DRAWER F
INDEPENDENCE, CALIFORNIA 93526
July 9, 1975


Fair Political Practices Commission
P. C. Box 807
926 J Street, Suite 522
Sacramento, California 95807

Attn: Nan Hambleton, Information Officer

Gentlemen:

Under the Uniform District Election law, there is a provision
that if no more candidates file for office than there are
vacancies and no petition is filed requesting an election be
held, no election is held and candidates who have filed for
office are appointed and serve as though elected. (See Elections
Code Section 23520.)

The above situation is very common in Inyo County, and we would
appreciate a letter from you advising as to whether candidates
in these circumstances are required to file Campaign Statements
under the Political Reform Act.

We would appreciate hearing from you as soon as possible as
filing for the UDEL elections begins on August 7th and we need
to have the proper information to disseminate to candidates.

Very truly yours

MARGARET BROMLEY
County Clerk

MB:tq

sections.question: whether a person who does not appear on the ballot is considered a candidate and must file campaign statements
sections.conclusion: Section 82007 of the Government Code defines the term candidate as 'an individual who is listed on the ballot ... or who receives a contribution or makes an expenditure or gives his consent for any other person to receive a contribution or make an expenditure with a view to bringing about his nomination or election to any elective office ...'. Thus, the candidates described in your letter will not have to file campaign statements if they do not appear on the ballot and do not receive contributions or make expenditures.
sections.question_synthetic: The County Clerk of Inyo County asked whether candidates who do not appear on the ballot but are appointed to office under the Uniform District Election Law are required to file campaign statements under the Political Reform Act.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declined to issue a formal opinion because the question did not raise a substantial question of interpretation. However, they clarified that candidates not appearing on the ballot are not required to file campaign statements unless they receive contributions, make expenditures, or give consent for others to do so.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a declination letter (formal opinion denied) but includes substantive guidance. The document includes both the incoming request letter and the FPPC response. Very clear question and conclusion sections present.

CONCLUSION: Section 82007 of the Government Code defines the term candidate as 'an individual who is listed on the ballot ... or who receives a contribution or makes an expenditure or gives his consent for any other person to receive a contribution or make an expenditure with a view to bringing about his nomination or election to any elective office ...'. Thus, the candidates described in your letter will not have to file campaign statements if they do not appear on the ballot and do not receive contributions or make expenditures.

Box 807 926 J Street, Suite 522 Sacramento, California 95807 Attn: Nan Hambleton, Information Officer Gentlemen: Under the Uniform District Election law, there is a provision that if no more candidates file for office than there are vacancies and no petition is filed requesting an election be held, no election is held and candidates who have filed for office are appointed and serve as though elected. Very truly yours MARGARET BROMLEY County Clerk MB:tq
embedding.summary: The FPPC declined to issue a formal opinion to the Inyo County Clerk regarding whether appointed candidates (under the Uniform District Election Law) must file campaign statements, but provided guidance that such candidates need not file if they do not appear on the ballot and do not receive contributions or make expenditures.
